---
title: "Quone in Quarto and knitr"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Quone in Quarto and knitr}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  eval = FALSE
)
```

```{r setup}
library(quone)
```

`quone` registers a `knitr` engine for `quone` chunks on load.
Chunks like the one below compile to R, `source()` the result, and
optionally show the Quone source, the generated R, or both.

````
```{quone, quone.show = "both"}
scores <- [92.0, 88.5, 71.0, 64.0]
mean_score <- mean scores
```
````

The `quone.show` chunk option controls the rendered output:

- `"quone"` -- show only the Quone source (default in books).
- `"r"` -- show only the generated R.
- `"both"` -- side-by-side, mirroring the [quone-lang.org](https://quone-lang.org/)
  hero.

Set `eval = FALSE` to skip evaluation; chunk options behave the same
way as for an R chunk.
